_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an index averaging prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice with a 2015 base of 100, currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase since 2015. This rise reflects a recent and uneven climb rather than steady inflation across all items. The index captures global commodity price changes and helps track broad cost trends in common breakfast staples.
notes.movers: The primary contributors to the basket's increase are coffee, which has nearly doubled (+99%), and orange juice, up by 34%. Wheat and sugar show more modest increases of 19% and 13%, respectively. These shifts are driven largely by weather events and crop-specific diseases such as frost, drought in key coffee-growing areas, and citrus greening affecting orange juice production. The soft commodity markets involved are relatively thin, meaning that even localized disruptions significantly impact global prices, rather than general inflationary pressures.
notes.why: The basket’s rise is chiefly influenced by weather-sensitive crops vulnerable to climate and disease pressures. Such conditions can sustain elevated prices over extended periods, as supply adjustments tend to lag. Understanding these dynamics is critical in assessing future price trajectories in these commodity segments.
